today_stories.headline: Fed signals no rate cut until Q3
today_stories.explainer: Powell said inflation is sticky and the Fed needs more evidence before cutting. Rates stay at 4.50% for at least another two meetings. Bond yields up, growth stocks slipped slightly on the news.

today_stories.headline: NVIDIA datacentre revenue +85% — picks-and-shovels names quietly rally
today_stories.explainer: NVDA earnings confirmed AI infrastructure demand is real, but the big move came in Layer-2 names: Vertiv (cooling), Eaton (power), Vistra (utility) all up 4-9% in sympathy. Most retail saw NVDA — the layer below saw the gem.

today_stories.headline: China tightens rare-earth export licensing
today_stories.explainer: Beijing announced licensing requirements for processed rare-earth exports — covers neodymium, praseodymium, and dysprosium. Western producers like MP Materials (US) and Lynas (AU) saw immediate inflows. Defence supply chain is the second-order story.

ipo_of_week.name: Quantum Logic Systems
ipo_of_week.blurb: Builds error-correction software for quantum computing chips. Customers include IBM and Rigetti. Expected $4B valuation; Sequoia + a16z led the last private round at $3.2B in late 2025.
